meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement analytics to overcome a lack of portfolio experience. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to showcase real data.
content.answer_block: Transform your blank portfolio into a data-driven narrative by integrating analytics into your first project. By tracking real user interactions with Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift the conversation from 'what I built' to 'how my code impacted users,' proving your engineering competence to future employers immediately.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the silence of a zero-traffic dashboard echoing the anxiety of your first job hunt. The industry doesn't just want to see code; it wants to see impact, and without a legacy of shipped products, you feel invisible in a sea of senior candidates.

But here is the secret: you don't need a decade of experience to show value. You need a feedback loop. By integrating professional-grade analytics into your student project, you provide concrete evidence of your ability to measure, iterate, and optimize software. This isn't just a technical task; it's your competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify what success looks like for your project. Are you tracking button clicks, user retention, or feature adoption? This defines the 'why' behind your data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Help me define the key user interaction metrics for a SaaS project portfolio site. I want to track user engagement to demonstrate I understand product-led growth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a schema for tracking user interaction events on a static web app to show potential employers my analytical thinking.'

steps.title: Initialize Antigravity Integration
steps.description: Configure the SDK to ensure you aren't just logging noise, but actionable events. We will setup the environment variables for secure data transmission.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate integration for Antigravity analytics in a Next.js environment. Ensure the setup is modular and follows clean architecture patterns.

steps.title: Implement Event Hooks
steps.description: Strategic placement of tracking hooks is key. Don't track everything; track the moments that prove user intent and product functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom React hook called useAnalyticsEvent that sends event data to Antigravity, including timestamps and user session context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create src/hooks/useAnalyticsEvent.ts to handle event tracking with Antigravity SDK.'

steps.title: Visualize the User Journey
steps.description: Raw data is useless without a story. Build a simple dashboard view in your app that displays these metrics to prove you can interpret your own data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a simple dashboard component that fetches and displays the last 5 user events from the Antigravity API to show on an admin panel.

steps.title: Audit and Optimize
steps.description: Ensure your analytics don't degrade performance. We use Claude Code to run a performance check on the new tracking logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on my analytics implementation to ensure it doesn't block the main thread or negatively impact Core Web Vitals.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze current code for performance bottlenecks caused by the new analytics integration.'

pro_tips: Always track 'intent' events like 'Sign Up Click' instead of just 'Page Load' to show you care about conversion funnels.
Use environment variables for your API keys; exposing them in client-side code is a red flag for senior reviewers.
Include a 'Data Strategy' section in your README to explain exactly why you chose to track specific metrics.
Simulate traffic using a local test script to populate your dashboard before showing it to recruiters.
Annotate your code with comments explaining the 'why' behind specific tracking points to demonstrate senior-level intent.
content.next_steps: Once your dashboard is live, write a 'Case Study' blog post explaining one insight you gained from your analytics and how you changed your code to improve that metric.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a coder; you are a data-informed engineer with a portfolio that speaks for itself. The barrier to entry isn't your experience, it's your presentation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
